title: add EC2 SSH pre-flight check to rotation workflow
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Knock knock, EC2 there?
gist.blurb: Added a pre-flight SSH check to our infrastructure rotation workflow. Now we make sure the EC2 instance is awake and ready before we start swapping out tokens.
gist.summary: Added an EC2 SSH connectivity pre-flight check to the infrastructure token rotation workflow.
gist.impact: Prevents token rotation failures by verifying EC2 instance reachability and service health before initiating the rotation process.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about checking if a server (EC2) is awake and reachable before doing work. Visual: The bee mascot checking on a server rack or terminal, ensuring it's online. The monitor robot can be assisting with a green checkmark.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat, knocking on a retro server rack that glows with a soft lime green (#ecf874) light. A chunky monitor robot stands nearby giving a thumbs up. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat, knocking on a retro server rack that glows with a soft lime green (#ecf874) light.
